evals.prompt: Use $project-verifier in Full Suite mode on this Python CLI. It reformats local CSV files, has no model calls or external services, and I do not need interview materials.
evals.expected_output: Classify the project as non-AI, complete the offline core, treat Phase 5 as not_applicable, and do not create optional interview artifacts.
expectations: The manifest classifies the project as non-AI with repository evidence.
Phase 5 writes not_applicable artifacts without proposing an LLM comparison.
The agent offers the optional interview export and does not create interview_evidence_pack.md when declined.

evals.prompt: I have an AI application that needs MODEL_API_KEY, but the key and backend are not configured yet. Help me choose benchmark scenarios, but do not make any real calls.
evals.expected_output: Propose guided scenarios and a durable benchmark plan, identify blockers, offer repair versus plan-only, and generate no scores or live runners when plan-only is selected.
expectations: The agent proposes 3-5 project-specific directions and recommends 1-2.
Missing environment names and backend dependencies are listed without reading secret values.
The result is skipped or blocked with recovery conditions and contains no fabricated scores.

evals.prompt: This fixture has a deterministic local AI backend. Design L2 and L3 tests, but treat preflight as different from execution and ask before any full run.
evals.expected_output: Classify the feature as AI, define raw metrics and a fair local baseline, run only no-call preflight automatically, and wait for revision-bound execution authorization.
expectations: Preflight checks environment names, files, commands, runtimes, and task wiring without live calls.
The agent presents bounded calls, retries, timeouts, and side effects.
No live runner is executed before explicit execution authorization.

evals.prompt: Review this mixed product. The local normalization path is non-AI and the optional generation path is AI-assisted. Do not apply an AI benchmark to the whole project.
evals.expected_output: Classify features separately, keep L3 limited to the optional AI path, and preserve the non-AI path as ordinary L1/L2 verification.
expectations: The path register records different feature-level classifications.
The non-AI path is not forced into an LLM comparison.
The AI-assisted path still requires user-approved baseline, metrics, and budget.

evals.prompt: The project changed after I approved Phase 4. Use the attached manifest and authorization receipt, but do not ask me again unless the old approval is actually stale.
evals.expected_output: Detect the proposal hash mismatch, refuse execution before any live path, invalidate the stale decision, and request renewed approval.
expectations: No path runs with the stale receipt.
The mismatch is tied to proposal_sha256 or source_revision.
A new user decision is required; silence is not treated as approval.

evals.prompt: Run the attached local E2E fixture after a valid execution gate. One path succeeds and one fails. Do not call the workflow itself broken and do not describe the product path as fully passing.
evals.expected_output: Complete the authorized run with result_outcome partial, retain both path results, and keep claims bounded to the observed full-scope evidence.
expectations: phase_status is completed while result_outcome is partial.
The successful and failed paths remain visible with their exit codes.
The result does not claim that every P0 path passed.
No interview or public-claim artifact is generated without a separate gate.
